title: service-accounts — developer configuration
description: Config keys owned by the service-accounts module. JWT-signing settings themselves (algorithm, kid rotation cadence) live in `identity/auth/config/auth.php` under the `jwt.*` prefix — THIS module's `jwt.*` keys are the SA-specific overlay (TTL bounds, clock-skew tolerance). See design.md §6 (config keys owned by identity modules).

rotation.description: Mandatory-rotation policy. Applies to every SA regardless of tenant / application.

keys.description: Default secret TTL applied at creation + every rotation. Callers may override per-SA via CreateServiceAccountInput.expires_at OR the `--ttl` command flag.
Days before `expires_at` when NotifyRotationDueJob dispatches the reminder notification.
Days past `expires_at` before PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob soft-disables. Grace exists to catch operator-inflight rotations that finish shortly after expiry.

Additional email addresses (comma-separated in env) that receive the ServiceAccountSecretRotationDueNotification. Application owners are notified regardless — this list adds broadcast to team-wide DLs (e.g. `platform-ops@stackra.com`).

Days after soft-delete before hard-delete by PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob. Longer than typical retention because audit-correlation on machine actions extends further than human-actor retention (SOC 2 evidence window).

jwt.description: SA-specific JWT overlay on top of the auth module's global JWT config. TTL bounds enforce a floor + ceiling for `IssueTestJwtInput.ttl_seconds` and the runtime `requested_scope.ttl_seconds`.

keys.description: Default TTL for JWTs issued by the token-exchange endpoint when the request does not specify one. 5 minutes matches industry practice for machine-to-machine bearer tokens (short enough to bound compromise; long enough to survive intra-call latency).
Verifier tolerance for `iat` + `exp` claim drift. 30s absorbs typical NTP skew across pods without meaningfully extending the compromise window.

Upper bound. `IssueTestJwtInput.ttl_seconds` above this value refuses with 422 SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TTL_EXCEEDS_MAX. Long-TTL exceptions ship via the `service_accounts_extended_ttl` entitlement (see entitlements.json).
Lower bound. Requests below this refuse with 422 SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TTL_BELOW_MIN. Prevents callers from requesting near-zero TTLs that increase exchange-endpoint pressure.

naming.description: Naming policy for the `name` column. Enforced by `valid_service_account_name` rule.

keys.description: Max characters. Bounded so machine-generated names (e.g. `webhook-signer-{tenant_slug}-{integration}`) fit comfortably without truncation.
Regex pattern. Lowercase alphanumerics + hyphens + underscores; must start + end with alphanumeric. Rejects whitespace, punctuation, and Unicode to keep names URL-safe + shell-safe.

kid.description: signer_kid policy. Values flow through to the auth module's `jwt_signing_keys` table.

keys.description: `uuid` = kid is a UUIDv4 (kebab-case); `opaque` = kid is a bcrypt-friendly opaque string of exactly 32 chars. Consumers of the JWKS endpoint MUST NOT parse the kid — it's a lookup key, not structured data.

When true, generated kids are prefixed with the application slug (`app_sports-<uuid>`). Debug aid for operator triage; increases surface for information disclosure (kid names leak application identity into JWKS payloads). Default off.

When true, platform-plane SAs (`tenant_id IS NULL`) require a non-empty IP allowlist in metadata + the audit middleware refuses non-allowlisted source IPs. Enterprise-tier feature (see entitlements.json). Deferred to Wave 1b.
Days since `last_used_at` before DetectDormantServiceAccountJob flags the SA.
Multiplier over rolling-hour baseline that constitutes a rate-spike anomaly. Combined with novel-tuple detection to fire SuspiciousServiceAccountActivityNotification.
When true, every mutating Artisan command must be invoked with --reason=<text> (audit-logged). Off in dev; on in staging + prod.
